end time.documentation: The value of simulated time where the simulation ends. Overrides final increment if greater than 0.

final increment.documentation: The final increment for the simulation.

global refinement.documentation: The number of initial refinements of the coarse mesh.

mesh type.documentation: The type of mesh to use.

read initial conditions from file.documentation: Whether to read any initial conditions from file.

remeshing period.documentation: The number of time steps between mesh refinement operations.

start time.documentation: The value of simulated time where the simulation begins.

time step.documentation: The time step size for the simulation.

x lower bound.documentation: The lower bound of the domain in the x-direction.

y lower bound.documentation: The lower bound of the domain in the y-direction.

z lower bound.documentation: The lower bound of the domain in the z-direction.

time dependent.documentation: Whether these conditions vary in time.

variables.documentation: The names of the fields that will use these constraints.

condition.documentation: The spacing type for outputting the solution fields.

directory.documentation: The name of the output directory.

file name.documentation: The prefix of the output files, before the time step and processor info are added.

list.documentation: The list of time steps to output. Used for the LIST type only and must be comma delimited.

load from checkpoint.documentation: Whether to load from a checkpoint created during a previous simulation.

number.documentation: The number of outputs (or number of outputs per decade for the N_PER_DECADE type).

file name.documentation: The file name to load from for each variable.

file variables.documentation: The names of the fields in the file.

format.documentation: The type of grid in the file.

simulation variables.documentation: The correspond names of the fields in the simulation.

x data points.documentation: The number of data points in the x-direction.

y data points.documentation: The number of data points in the y-direction.

z data points.documentation: The number of data points in the z-direction.

max iterations.documentation: The maximum number of linear solver iterations before the loop is stopped.

mg depth.documentation: The depth of the multigrid hierarchy.

preconditioner type.documentation: The preconditioner type for the linear solver.

solver type.documentation: The type of iterative solver to use for linear solves.

solver_ids.documentation: The ids of the solvers that will use these settings.

tolerance type.documentation: The tolerance type for the linear solver.

tolerance value.documentation: The value of for the linear solver tolerance.

exact residual.documentation: Flag for exact computation of residual.

eigenvalue cg iterations.documentation: The maximum number of CG iterations used to find the maximum eigenvalue.

smoothing range.documentation: The smoothing range for eigenvalues. Try λ_max / λ_min.

batched mode.documentation: Whether to use batched mode in GMRES.

force re-orthogonalization.documentation: Whether to force re-orthogonalization of the Krylov basis in GMRES.

max basis size.documentation: The maximum size of the Krylov basis used in GMRES before restarting.

orthogonalization strategy.documentation: The orthogonalization strategy to use in GMRES.

use default residual.documentation: Whether to use the default residual computation in GMRES.

use preconditioned residual.documentation: Whether to use the preconditioned residual l2 norm in the stopping criterion.

random seed.documentation: The random seed for the simulation. This is used to initialize the random number generator.

max iterations.documentation: The maximum number of nonlinear solver iterations before the loop is stopped.

solver_ids.documentation: The ids of the solvers that will use these settings.

step size.documentation: The constant damping value to be used if the backtrace line-search approach isn't used.

tolerance value.documentation: The value of for the nonlinear solver tolerance.

nucleation period.documentation: The number of increments between nucleation attempts.

refinement radius.documentation: The radius around a nucleus in which AMR is applied.

seeding increments.documentation: The number of increments over which nuclei are considered "active" and refinement and exclusion zones are applied. Same as "seeding time" but in increments.

seeding time.documentation: The time duration over which nuclei are considered "active" and refinement and exclusion zones are applied. Same as "seeding increments" but in time.

condition.documentation: The spacing type for outputting the solution fields.

directory.documentation: The name of the output directory.

file name.documentation: The prefix of the output files, before the time step and processor info are added.

file type.documentation: The output file type (either vtu, pvtu, vtk, or xdmf).

list.documentation: Comma-separated list of increments to output on.

number.documentation: The number of outputs for EQUAL_SPACING and for LOG_SPACING or number of outputs per decade for N_PER_DECADE.

subdivisions.documentation: The number of subdivisions to apply to the mesh when building output patches. If 0, the degree is used.

variables.documentation: The list of the fields to output. Must be comma delimited. Additionally, for the output of left-hand side and old fields, they must follow the same delimiters that are used in dependency sets. In other words, something like `set variables = n1, old_1(n1), lhs(n1)`.

gradient magnitude lower bound.documentation: The magnitude of the gradient above which the mesh should be refined.

type.documentation: The type of criterion used to determine if a cell should be refined. The options are none, value, gradient, value_and_gradient.

value lower bound.documentation: The lower bound for the window determining where the mesh should be refined.

value upper bound.documentation: The upper bound for the window determining where the mesh should be refined.

variables.documentation: The names of the fields that will use this refinement criterion.
